data.title: Brainstorm approved an epic plan that explicitly violated the zero-references success criterion
data.description:
## Observed Behavior
Epic 3601-f085 has a clear success criterion: 'grep -r "plugins/dso" plugins/dso/ returns zero matches across the distributed plugin tree.' Despite this, the brainstorm and preplanning phases produced stories that explicitly planned to LEAVE plugins/dso references in place via annotation bypass:

- S2 (af9e-77fc) done definition: 'grep/glob patterns that match against git-relative paths are annotated with plugin-self-ref-ok and excluded from the zero-occurrence count'
- S3 (ccc3-954a) consideration: 'grep/git commands in documentation referencing repo-relative paths — may stay as-is'
- S4 (7eb8-af48) done definition: 'grep -rn plugins/dso plugins/dso/ returns only lines annotated with plugin-self-ref-ok (zero unannotated matches)'
- S1 (075a-b781) verification test committed with a grep -v 'plugin-self-ref-ok' bypass that would hide surviving references

The success criterion says ZERO matches. The plan says 'zero matches after excluding annotated lines.' These are contradictory. The plan should have been rejected during brainstorm's fidelity review or preplanning's adversarial review.

## Expected Behavior
When a success criterion says 'zero matches,' the scrutiny pipeline (gap analysis, fidelity reviewers, red/blue team) should flag any story that plans to leave matches in place — regardless of whether those matches are annotated, exempted, or categorized as exceptions. The annotation strategy directly contradicts SC1 and should have been caught.

## Actual Behavior
The annotation strategy passed through brainstorm's 3-reviewer fidelity check, preplanning's adversarial review (which accepted 5/6 findings but did not flag this contradiction), and the approach-decision-maker's evaluation without any reviewer identifying the SC1 conflict.

## Root Cause
The brainstorm and preplanning reviewers accepted 'zero unannotated matches' as equivalent to 'zero matches' — a semantic gap. The scrutiny pipeline does not cross-reference story done definitions against the epic's success criteria to detect dilution (redefining 'zero' to mean 'zero after exclusions').

## Fix Direction
Add a 'criteria dilution check' to the epic scrutiny pipeline (Step 2.5 gap analysis): for each success criterion containing an absolute term (zero, all, every, none, no), verify that no story done definition introduces a qualifier that weakens the absolute (e.g., 'zero unannotated' weakening 'zero'). Flag as a CRITICAL gap if found.
